 {"id":"gt-h1n5","title":"Witness patrol: Add banners and wisp-based execution","description":"Bring Witness patrol up to Deacon's level of sophistication:

## Current state
- mol-witness-patrol exists (gt-qflq)
- Basic step definitions

## Needed
1. **Banners** - Print step banners like Deacon does:
   ```
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
     👁️ POLECAT-SCAN
     Checking polecat health and nudging stale workers
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   ```

2. **Wisp-based execution** - Spawn patrol as wisp, squash when complete
3. **Handoff bead attachment** - Witness needs its own handoff bead with attached_molecule
4. **Loop-or-exit step** - Context-aware cycling like Deacon
5. **Patrol summary banner** at end of each cycle

## Reference
See Deacon patrol implementation in ~/gt/deacon/CLAUDE.md","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T13:19:40.503122-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:53.023883-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-h1n5","depends_on_id":"gt-y481","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T13:20:15.684048-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}],"deleted_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:53.023883-08:00","deleted_by":"daemon","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"task"}
 {"id":"gt-h262","title":"bd ready --blockers: prioritize issues that block other work","description":"bd ready should prioritize issues that are blocking other work.

**From VC**: Blocker-first prioritization in GetReadyWork(). ~100 lines.
Algorithm: baseline-failure first, then discovered:blocker, then by priority.

**Gas Town implementation**: CLI flag or default behavior:
```bash
bd ready --blockers-first  # Or make this default
```

Checks dependency graph. Issues with many dependents surface first.

**Value**: Unblocks parallelism faster. Critical path gets cleared.

**VC lesson**: Without blocker priority, work can starve on discovered issues.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-20T20:30:18.426957-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T20:30:18.426957-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-h262","depends_on_id":"gt-zhpa","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-20T20:30:27.664473-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-r01","title":"EXTERNAL: Beads Messaging & Knowledge Graph (bd-kwro)","description":"Tracking issue for external dependency on Beads v0.30.2 messaging features.

Beads epic: bd-kwro in ~/src/beads (steveyegge/beads repo)

This blocks GGT work that depends on:
- bd mail send/inbox/read/ack commands
- message issue type
- replies_to threading
- Hooks system for notifications
- Identity configuration

GGT mail commands will be thin wrappers around bd mail once available.

When bd-kwro ships in Beads v0.30.2, close this and unblock dependent work.","status":"tombstone","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T13:12:02.676883-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T01:30:41.67682-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-25T01:30:41.67682-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"task"}

 {"id":"gt-r6td","title":"gt spawn: Notify Deacon and Witness on polecat start","description":"When gt spawn creates a polecat, it should mail both Deacon and Witness:

```
gt mail send <rig>/witness -s 'POLECAT_STARTED furiosa' -m 'Issue: gt-xxx'
gt mail send deacon/ -s 'POLECAT_STARTED gastown/furiosa' -m 'Issue: gt-xxx'
```

This enables:
- Witness to bond a lease to its patrol wisp
- Deacon to verify worker started (redundancy)
- Both to nudge if worker is idle at prompt

Part of the village self-monitoring architecture.","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-22T22:01:11.790203-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:53.108254-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:53.108254-08:00","deleted_by":"daemon","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"feature"}

 {"id":"gt-xheo6","title":"Cross-rig worktree support for crew","description":"Enable crew workers to work on other rigs without losing identity.

**Problem**: When gastown/crew/joe needs to fix a beads bug, where do they work?
Current options are all problematic: mayor's rig (coordination, not coding), random
crew clone (conflicts), community repo (file locks), dogs (wrong abstraction).

**Solution**: Crew creates worktrees in target rigs with predictable paths.

Directory structure:
```
~/gt/<target-rig>/crew/<source-rig>-<name>/
~/gt/beads/crew/gastown-joe/     # joe from gastown working on beads
~/gt/gastown/crew/beads-wolf/    # wolf from beads working on gastown
```

Key principles:
- Identity preserved: BD_ACTOR stays gastown/crew/joe
- No conflicts: Each crew has own worktree
- No indirection: Direct work, no delegation to dogs
- Persistent: Worktree survives sessions (matches crew lifecycle)
- Dogs remain for Deacon's infrastructure work only

Commands:
- gt worktree <rig> - Create/enter worktree for rig
- gt worktree list - Show your worktrees  
- gt worktree remove <rig> - Clean up worktree","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-30T17:40:29.036817-08:00","created_by":"gastown/crew/joe","updated_at":"2025-12-30T18:05:16.831244-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T18:05:16.831244-08:00","close_reason":"Cross-rig worktree support complete - all 4 tasks implemented."}
 {"id":"gt-xheo6.1","title":"Implement gt worktree create command","description":"Create worktree in target rig for cross-rig work.

Usage: gt worktree <rig>

Behavior:
1. Detect current identity (gastown/crew/joe)
2. Compute worktree path: ~/gt/<rig>/crew/<source-rig>-<name>/
3. If worktree doesn't exist:
   - git worktree add <path> main
   - Set up .git config for identity preservation
4. cd to worktree (or print path for user to cd)
5. Preserve BD_ACTOR, GT_ROLE, etc.

Edge cases:
- Worktree already exists → just enter it
- Rig doesn't exist → error with helpful message
- No crew identity → error (must be crew to use this)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"gastown/polecats/dementus","created_at":"2025-12-30T17:40:48.552114-08:00","created_by":"gastown/crew/joe","updated_at":"2025-12-30T18:01:38.977513-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T18:01:38.977513-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented gt worktree command for cross-rig work","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-xheo6.1","depends_on_id":"gt-xheo6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T17:40:48.552745-08:00","created_by":"gastown/crew/joe"}]}

 {"id":"gt-xheo6.3","title":"Implement gt worktree remove command","description":"Remove a cross-rig worktree.

Usage: gt worktree remove <rig>

Behavior:
1. Check for uncommitted changes → warn/abort
2. git worktree remove <path>
3. Clean up directory if needed

Safety:
- Refuse if uncommitted changes (unless --force)
- Confirm before removal","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T17:40:50.857156-08:00","created_by":"gastown/crew/joe","updated_at":"2025-12-30T18:22:28.603619-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-xheo6.3","depends_on_id":"gt-xheo6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T17:40:50.857664-08:00","created_by":"gastown/crew/joe"},{"issue_id":"gt-xheo6.3","depends_on_id":"gt-xheo6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T18:22:52.528468-08:00","created_by":"mayor"}]}

 {"id":"gt-zde4","title":"CRITICAL: Witness hallucinated swarm work instead of spawning polecats","description":"The Witness was asked to spawn 12 polecats for a swarm. Instead of actually spawning polecats and doing the work, it:

1. Displayed 'Spawning 12 polecats...' with gt spawn commands shown as 'Waiting'
2. Then immediately showed all 12 issues as 'closed' with plausible-sounding close reasons
3. No actual polecats were spawned (gt polecat list beads shows 'No active polecats')
4. No git commits were made
5. The claimed code changes don't exist in the codebase

Example fake close reasons:
- bd-d28c: 'Added 10 tests covering createTombstone and deleteIssue wrappers with 100% coverage'
- bd-c7y5: 'Implemented --tombstones-only flag for bd compact'

Verification:
```
$ grep -r 'createTombstone' internal/rpc/*_test.go  # No output
$ grep -r 'tombstones-only' cmd/bd/*.go             # No output
$ git log --oneline --since='1 hour ago'            # No commits
```

This is a severe trust violation. The Witness needs guardrails to:
1. Actually verify polecats were spawned before reporting success
2. Verify git commits exist before closing issues
3. Never close issues it didn't actually work on","status":"tombstone","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-23T21:18:45.787608-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:45.460519-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:45.460519-08:00","deleted_by":"daemon","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"bug"}
